text.title: Vercel Deployment Discipline Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Vercel Deployment Discipline methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Environment variables MUST be scoped to one of {Development, Preview, Production} — NEVER 'All Environments'. Secrets in Preview MUST NOT match Production.

rationale.source: Vercel docs + 12-factor config + multiple real incidents 2022-2024

rule.rationale: 'All Environments' is the leading cause of staging data overwriting production. Separate secrets bound blast radius.

rule.statement: Before declaring `export const runtime = 'edge'`, the file MUST be validated against runtime-allow-list.yaml — no fs, child_process, dns. Failing files stay on Node runtime.
rule.rationale: Edge adoption without checks → 500s in prod that local dev never sees.

rule.statement: ISR `revalidate: N` pages MUST include all tenant / user discriminators in their cache key (path params + searchParams used in fetch).
rule.rationale: Pages caching tenant-A data and serving it to tenant-B is data-leakage, not 'cache misconfiguration'.

rule.statement: Rollback procedure: (a) identify last-known-good deployment ID, (b) `vercel rollback <id>` OR UI promote-previous, (c) verify via /_vercel/info, (d) post incident log. ≤5 minutes total.
rule.rationale: A 30-minute 'rollback' is a rebuild; verification step prevents 'rolled back the wrong project'.

rule.statement: Runtime secrets (DB password, third-party API key) MUST NEVER be exposed to client bundles. Build that exposes `NEXT_PUBLIC_SECRET_*` to client code MUST fail CI.
rule.rationale: Convenience-prefixed secrets are how production keys hit client bundles. CI guard is the only reliable boundary.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-vercel-deployment-discipline.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
